topic: Second-by-second retention mechanics in short-form vertical video
caveat: Multiplier stats (4.2x impressions, caption lift) come from creator-marketing blogs citing unlinked internal data - directional, not ground truth. Highest-confidence: vidIQ first-party channel figures and YouTube's 2025-03-31 loop-counting policy.

findings.claim: The steepest drop on a short-form retention curve is the first 3 seconds; roughly 50-60% of all viewers who abandon a Short do so within the first three seconds, setting the ceiling for the entire curve.
Meta's internal research (cited) found the median scroll-or-stay decision happens at ~3.1 seconds.
Auto-looping makes short-form retention roughly double long-form: vidIQ first-party 73.6% average-percentage-viewed on Shorts vs 30.3% long-form.
Functional intro-retention target: >70% of viewers still watching past second 3; below ~40% the hook is failing regardless of the rest.
Secondary mid-video sag around 25-35s; top editors place a deliberate pattern interrupt there.
High-retention Shorts average one cut / visual event every 2-4 seconds; a meaningful visual shift every 2-3s is the recommended cadence.
Cut intervals should be VARIED, not metronomic: fixed-interval cuts habituate and stop working; alternate e.g. 3s, 5s, 2.5s.
Successful short-form inverts story structure: lead with the payoff/answer in the first 1-3s; time-to-first-payoff near zero.
Bold-claim and curiosity-gap hooks outperform; recurring high-hold openers: cold-open mid-action, question, visual anomaly, countdown/promise. No logo/title card in first 3s.
Burned-in captions raise retention ~15-25% (majority watch muted); captions present within the first 3 seconds.
Text overlays: ~4-6 words max, appearing ~200-400ms after the key phrase begins, displayed ~1.5-2.5s.

Loops are a primary tail mechanic: seamless ending flowing back into the opening (match cut, callback line, continuous audio) drives replays the algorithm weights heavily. Hard stops/black frames/freeze frames break loops.
End-card CTAs hurt short-form completion (a 3s follow-for-more is 15% of a 20s runtime); CTA goes in the caption.
Since 2025-03-31 each Shorts loop counts as an additional view; replay rate ~10% is excellent, 15-20%+ strong.
By 2025-26 completion + rewatch overtook the 3-second view as dominant ranking signals; viral completion bar ~70%.
Length vs completion: <15s = 60-75% avg retention; 15-30s = 55-65%; 30-60s = 40-55%; 60-90s Reels 30-45%. Sweet spot 20-25s (our 60-75s format accepts the lower band as a format tradeoff).

actionable_rules: Show the payoff subject on screen within the first 1.5 seconds; time-to-first-payoff < 3s.
Open with a tagged hook type: cold-open mid-action, question, visual anomaly, or countdown/promise. No brand card in the first 3s.
A new visual event at least every 3 seconds; no static shot over 4 seconds in the first 15s.
Vary cut intervals; no exact interval repeated 3+ times in a row.
Deliberate pattern interrupt at the 25-35s mark.
Captions burned in from second 1; overlays <= 6 words, 1.5-2.5s on screen, cut early not late.
Loopable ending: final frame match-cuts/callbacks to the opening, audio continuous, no black/freeze tail.
No spoken end-card CTA; ending <= last 10% of runtime.
